Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]]. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Sasebo City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a [[:en:Oracle|Oracle]] led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == *[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] *[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] *[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] *[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] *[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]]. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Sasebo City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a [[:en:Oracle|Oracle]] led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == *[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] *[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] *[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] *[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] *[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]]. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Sasebo City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a [[:en:Oracle|Oracle]] led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == *[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] *[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] *[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] *[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] *[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]]. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Sasebo City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a [[:en:Oracle|Oracle]] led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == *[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] *[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] *[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] *[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] *[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370833]] Wikidata name: Kameyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]]. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Sasebo City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a [[:en:Oracle|Oracle]] led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == *[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] *[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] *[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] *[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] *[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *[[:en:Saihi Motor|Saihi Motor]] (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *[[:en:Matsuura Railway|Matsuura Railway]] {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of shrines]] *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=Oracle}} led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == *{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} *{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi}} == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *{{ill|Saihi Motor|en|Saihi Motor|WD=Q7400098|lt=Saihi Motor}} (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *{{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway}} {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|WD=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Sasebo City Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=Oracle}} led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == *{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} *{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi}} == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *{{ill|Saihi Motor|en|Saihi Motor|WD=Q7400098|lt=Saihi Motor}} (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *{{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway}} {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Sasebo Kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|WD=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | annual_festival={{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|1=Sasebo}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City|1=Sasebo}}. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Q11382539|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=Oracle|1=Oracle}} led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == *{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} *{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *{{ill|Saihi Motor|en|Saihi Motor|WD=Q7400098|lt=Saihi Motor|1=Saihi Bus}} (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *{{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway|1=Matsuura Railway}} {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|WD=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=Hachiman}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|WD=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=Hachiman}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | annual_festival={{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|1=Sasebo}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City|1=Sasebo}}. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Q11382539|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}}) when a {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=Oracle|1=Oracle}} led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == *{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} *{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *{{ill|Saihi Motor|en|Saihi Motor|WD=Q7400098|lt=Saihi Motor|1=Saihi Bus}} (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *{{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|WD=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway|1=Matsuura Railway}} {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|WD=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=Hachiman}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}}) when a {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|qid=Q217123|lt=Oracle|1=Oracle}} led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == *{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} *{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *{{ill|Saihi Motor|en|Saihi Motor|qid=Q7400098|lt=Saihi Motor|1=Saihi Bus}} (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *{{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|qid=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway|1=Matsuura Railway}} {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qid=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=Hachiman}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)]][[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kameyama Hachimangu | image=File:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg | location=Nagasaki Prefecture, Sasebo City, Hachiman Town 3-3 | coordinates={{Coord|33.18188888888889|N|129.71722222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} | Shrine_Rank=Prefectural shrine・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | annual_festival={{ill|October 15|en|October 15|qid=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Kameyama Hachimangu|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | prefecture={{ill|Sasebo|qid=Q328000|1=Sasebo}} }} '''Kameyama Hachimangu''' (かめやまはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|qid=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City|1=Sasebo}}. Known locally as "Hachiman Shrine" or "Hachiman-sama," it is the largest shrine in the city. == Overview == The shrine is located in the center of Sasebo City, northeast of the {{ill|Sasebo City Hall|ja|佐世保市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo City Hall|1=Q11382539|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382539}} and across from National Route 204. It is the only {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} in the city. Within the grounds, there are a total of seven auxiliary shrines, including the Kameyama Inari Shrine. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it began in the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} ({{ill|Hakuhō period|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}}) when a {{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|qid=Q217123|lt=Oracle|1=Oracle}} led to the transfer of spirits from [[Usa Jingū]] From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} onwards, it became a guardian shrine for the naval personnel of the {{ill|Sasebo Naval District|ja|軍港佐世保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Naval District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Sasebo Air Raid|ja|佐世保空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Air Raid|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 佐世保大空襲|1=Bombing of Sasebo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382525}} on June 29, 1945. The current buildings were rebuilt in 1964 using reinforced concrete. Before World War II, it was finally Ranked as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and after the war, it became a Beppyo shrine of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == *{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} *{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} *{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} == Events == [[File:Kameyama hachimangu shrine Summer festival.JPG|thumbnail|right|Kameyama Hachimangu Summer festival]] *Hatsumode, Coming-of-Age ceremony, Omiyamairi, {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382501}} *Summer festival - Held from August 1 to 3. Stalls and vendors are set up along the approach, and dances are performed on the stage within the shrine grounds. Until around 2010, fireworks were launched from the grounds of the adjacent {{ill|Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|ja|長崎県立佐世保北中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|1=Sasebo Kita Junior High and High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11652682}} at 9 PM on the first and last days, and Niagara fireworks were lit within the shrine grounds. == Access == *{{ill|Saihi Motor|en|Saihi Motor|qid=Q7400098|lt=Saihi Motor|1=Saihi Bus}} (including {{ill|Sasebo Bus|ja|させぼバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Bus|1=Sasebo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11267964}}) Get off at "Sasebo City Hall" *{{ill|Matsuura Railway|en|Matsuura Railway|qid=Q6788223|lt=Matsuura Railway|1=Matsuura Railway}} {{ill|Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja|西九州線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Kyushu Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松浦鉄道西九州線|1=Nishi-Kyūshū Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7040371}} Get off at "Kita-Sasebo Station", 8 minutes on foot == See also == *{{ill|Sasebo Kunchi|ja|佐世保くんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasebo Kunchi|1=Q11382501|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382501}} - A festival centered around the shrine's grand festival held every year from November 1 to 3. *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|en|List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qid=Q2517815|lt=List of shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} *{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman shrine|1=Hachiman}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)|version=102020420|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Kameyama hachimangu Sasebo.jpg|250px]] [[ja:亀山八幡宮_(佐世保市)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama Hachiman-gū (Sasebo)}} [[ja:亀山八幡宮 (佐世保市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370833}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sasebo, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
